key_requirements: R1: Kwargs arg count fix in codegen — HIR→MIR emits kwargs as trailing positional args to match runtime arity
R2: No-arg constructor guard — list()/tuple()/set()/bytes() zero-arg route to _new variants
R3: Print sep/end kwargs runtime — mb_print_kwargs(args, sep, end)
R4: Sorted/min/max/sum kwargs runtime — key function, reverse flag, default, start accumulator
R5: Numeric builtins — pow(x,y,mod) three-arg form, int(s, base) radix conversion
R6: chr/ord full Unicode range via char::from_u32
R7: Parse inline dict/set literals in try body — expr.rs/expr_compound.rs
R8: str.format() keyword argument substitution via {name} placeholders
R9: Generator send TypeError on just-started generator (state==Created)
R10: Generator throw propagation; StopIteration on exhausted generator
R11: Generator close no-op on exhausted; RuntimeError if yields after GeneratorExit
R12: iter(callable, sentinel) two-arg form; zip/map/filter accept generator iterators
R13: isinstance(x, tuple-of-types); getattr(obj, attr, default) three-arg form
Conformance harness: # mamba-xfail: <reason> directive marks expected failures
Golden files: .py + .expected pairs, regenerated via python3 tests/regen_golden.py
R1: GeneratorState enum — Created/Running/Suspended/Completed state machine
R2: yield suspension/resumption — local variables preserved in heap frame
R4: send(value) — yield expression evaluates to sent value; first send must be None (TypeError otherwise)
R5: throw(exc)/close() — inject exception at yield point; GeneratorExit on close; no-op on Completed
close() must raise RuntimeError if generator catches GeneratorExit and yields again
throw() on Completed state must raise StopIteration
R1: mb_iter_new/mb_iter_next — __iter__/__next__ dispatch, StopIteration terminates loop
R2: Built-in iterator variants — ListIter, DictIter, SetIter, TupleIter
R3: Iterator combinators — zip(*iters), map(fn, iter), filter(fn, iter) accepting generator iterators
iter(callable, sentinel) two-argument form — call callable until result == sentinel
R5: next(iter, default) — returns default instead of raising StopIteration
R2: Aggregation — sorted(iter, key, reverse), min(iter, key, default), max(iter, key, default), sum(iter, start)
R3: isinstance(obj, classinfo) — accept tuple-of-types as second arg via MRO walk
R4: chr(n) full Unicode via char::from_u32; ord(c) full Unicode codepoint
R5: pow(x, y, mod) three-arg modular exponentiation; int(s, base) radix conversion
getattr(obj, attr, default) optional third argument — return default on missing attr
R6: Symbol registration — kwargs-aware variants registered in symbols.rs with correct arity
R4: mb_string_format(template, args, argc) — f-string and str.format() runtime formatting
Extend mb_string_format for {name} keyword placeholder substitution from kwargs dict
R1: Core string methods — split, join, strip, replace, find, startswith, endswith, upper, lower
CallExtern emission — when HIR Call has kwargs and target is a known builtin, emit kwargs as trailing positional args
builtin_extern_map entries for _kwargs variants with correct param counts
Zero-arg arity guard — when args.is_empty() and extern is _from_iterable/_from_pairs, redirect to _new variant
MIR::CallExtern { func: '_kwargs variant', args: [positional ++ kwargs_values] }
JIT symbol table registration — mb_print_kwargs, mb_sorted_kwargs, mb_min_kwargs, mb_max_kwargs, mb_sum_kwargs
Each kwargs symbol registered with correct param count matching extended runtime signature
JIT_LOCK serializes compilation+execution across test threads (SIGBUS prevention on aarch64)
Function signature generation — declare signatures with extended params for kwargs variants
Call site emission — emit call instruction with matching arg count so Cranelift verifier passes
Codegen emits mb_getattr/mb_setattr for all user-class attribute access (descriptor protocol path)
parse_expr_stmt — try dict/set literal when '{' encountered at statement position before block fallback
Dict literal {key: val} and set literal {expr, expr} in compound expression contexts (e.g., inside try body)
Allows {}['x'] and {1,2}.remove(99) as expression statements inside try blocks
Postfix parsing: subscript/method call after dict/set literal — {}['x'] and {1,2}.remove(99)
Exception chaining — __cause__ attribute set when 'raise X from Y' used
Exception chaining — __context__ set implicitly during exception handling
Custom exception classes — inheritance, super().__init__(), custom attributes
R1: Type-tagged dispatch — mb_call_method routes by ObjData variant tag
R2: MRO fallback — C3 MRO attribute lookup for ObjData::Instance
MRO introspection — __mro__ attribute access, staticmethod/classmethod dispatch
R9: Magic method dispatch — __len__, __contains__, __iter__, __repr__, __str__
# mamba-xfail: <reason> directive — marks fixture as expected failure, skipped in CI
R1: RUN directive dispatch — parse/typecheck/jit runners
R3: Recursive fixture discovery — auto-discovers .py under tests/fixtures/conformance/
Conformance test: parse + typecheck + JIT execute + compare stdout vs .expected golden
math.floor, math.ceil, math.sqrt, math.factorial, math.gcd — basic functions
math.log, math.log2, math.log10 — logarithmic functions (partial support gap)
math.sin, math.cos, math.tan — trigonometric (partial support gap)
math.pi, math.e, math.inf, math.nan — constants
itertools.chain, itertools.islice, itertools.product, itertools.combinations, itertools.permutations
itertools.groupby, itertools.takewhile, itertools.dropwhile, itertools.zip_longest
Fixture simplification: test only implemented itertools functions; skip unimplemented
functools.reduce, functools.partial, functools.lru_cache, functools.wraps
functools.cache, functools.cached_property
Fixture simplification: test only implemented functools functions
collections.defaultdict, collections.Counter, collections.OrderedDict, collections.deque
collections.namedtuple — factory for tuple subclasses with named fields
Fixture simplification: test only implemented collections types
